concerns.analysis: The source document features a meaningful agency logo ('NC Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY') on the first page. Although the HTML contains an tag for this logo, the provided image manifest explicitly indicates that the base64 extraction failed for this image ('valid': false, 'error': 'No module named 'PIL''). According to the evaluation criteria, a corrupt or truncated image base64 that causes an image to fail to render constitutes a broken image. Because this affects the primary age
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Agency logo on the top left of page 1 ('NC Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY')
concerns.output_quote: Image manifest shows '"valid": false' and '"error": "No module named 'PIL'"' for the logo on page 1, meaning the image data is corrupt and will not render.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document makes extensive use of underlining for emphasis (e.g., the 'MEMORANDUM' title and list item labels like 'Beneficial Reuse'). The output does not capture this underline emphasis at all.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: MEMORANDUM (underlined on page 1) and Beneficial Reuse (bold and underlined on page 3)
concerns.location: Page 1 heading and Page 3 first list item

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
